diagnosis: compare-two-configs: The agent used read_text_file twice instead of read_multiple_files. The read_text_file description framed the multiple-files case as conditional advice (“When you need to read two or more files…”) rather than a hard rule, so the agent did not realize that reading two configs sequentially still required read_multiple_files. create-new-file: The agent called create_directory before write_file once because the write_file description prohibited create_directory but did not explain that write_file handles parent directory creation automatically, leaving the agent unsure whether the directory needed to exist first. make-nested-directory: The agent invoked create_directory repeatedly for parent paths and the target path. The description stated it creates nested directories in a single call, but did not emphatically forbid calling it multiple times for the same path or its parents, so the agent kept redundantly calling it. move-into-archive: The agent called list_directory or create_directory before move_file. The move_file description only banned create_directory and get_file_info beforehand, so the agent treated list_directory as an allowed verification step and used it to check the destination or source before moving.

rationales.read_text_file: The agent was reading two files sequentially with read_text_file because the old description sounded advisory rather than prohibitive for sequential multi-file reads.
rationales.read_multiple_files: The agent preferred read_text_file for multi-file tasks, so the preference over read_text_file needs to be stated more strongly and explicitly tied to multi-file involvement.
rationales.write_file: The agent called create_directory before write_file because it did not know write_file handles parent directories automatically; explaining that removes the need to pre-create directories.
rationales.create_directory: The agent repeatedly called create_directory for parent paths and the target path because it did not trust that one call creates the entire nested path and did not realize re-calling the same path is unnecessary.
rationales.move_file: The agent called list_directory or create_directory before moving because the old description only prohibited create_directory and get_file_info, leaving list_directory as an apparently allowed verification step.

overrides.read_text_file: Use this ONLY to read a single text file. For two or more files — including when comparing, analyzing, or reading them sequentially — always use read_multiple_files instead. Use the 'head' or 'tail' parameter to read only the first or last N lines. Only works within allowed directories.
overrides.read_multiple_files: Use this to read two or more text files in a single call, such as when comparing or analyzing them. Always prefer this over read_text_file when multiple files are involved. Each file's content is returned with its path as a reference. Only works within allowed directories.
overrides.write_file: Use this to create a new text file or overwrite an existing one with the exact content provided. Automatically creates any missing parent directories. Do not call create_directory first. Only works within allowed directories.
overrides.create_directory: Use this only when the user explicitly asks to create a new directory or folder. Creates the full nested path including all missing parent directories in a single call. Do not call it for parent paths, and do not call it multiple times for the same path. Do not use it before write_file or move_file. Only works within allowed directories.
overrides.move_file: Use this to move or rename a file or directory by providing the source and full destination paths. The operation will fail if a file already exists at the destination. Do not call list_directory, get_file_info, or create_directory beforehand to verify the source or destination. Only works within allowed directories.
